evaluation.viewing_angle_source: The source figure shows a 3D stem plot from an oblique, elevated perspective. The camera is rotated such that the grid lines are not aligned with the screen diagonals, ensuring all 9 stems are clearly separated and visible without overlapping each other.
evaluation.viewing_angle_rendered: The rendered screenshot uses a 45-degree orthographic projection. Because of this exact 45-degree azimuth, the diagonal stems (like (-1,1), (0,0), and (1,-1)) align perfectly on the same vertical screen coordinates, causing them to visually overlap and appear stacked on top of each other.
evaluation.viewing_angle_comparison: The rendered camera angle has a major alignment issue: the 45-degree azimuth causes diagonal elements to overlap entirely, creating a confusing and incorrect visual representation of the 3D stem plot, whereas the source figure uses an offset angle where all stems are clearly distinct.
discrepancies: Perfect diagonal overlap of stems due to the 45-degree camera azimuth, making the plot look like a single vertical line of multiple dots in the center.
Axis labels (0, 1, -1) overlap with the grid lines instead of being cleanly offset outside the plotting area.
The camera viewpoint needs to be rotated slightly (e.g., azimuth of 30 or 60 degrees) to match the clear, non-overlapping perspective of the original.
evaluation.notes: The 3D stem plot suffers from severe visual overlap due to the 45-degree orthographic camera angle, and lacks any custom interactive controls.
action_items: Adjust the camera azimuth angle (e.g., to 30 or 60 degrees) to prevent stems from overlapping on the screen.
Add interactive UI elements, such as a slider to change the binomial filter order (e.g., from b1,1 to b3,3) to dynamically update the stems.
Reposition the axis tick labels further outside the grid boundaries to avoid overlapping the grid geometry.
Make the stem lines thinner to closer match the delicate lines of the textbook plot.

feedback.rationale: The rendered figure has severe visual overlap issues due to an incorrect 45-degree camera angle, overlaps in labels, and lacks required interactivity, which are all addressable in a refinement step.

evaluation.viewing_angle_source: The source figure shows a 3D discrete stem plot from an elevated perspective. The vertical axis rises on the left, the m-axis extends to the bottom-left, and the n-axis extends to the bottom-right, creating a balanced isometric-like view of a 3x3 grid on the horizontal plane.
evaluation.viewing_angle_rendered: The rendered screenshot shows a similar elevated 3D perspective, but it is zoomed out, creating excess whitespace. The axes are skewed, and the vertical axis is shifted more towards the center-left rather than framing the left edge cleanly.
evaluation.viewing_angle_comparison: The rendered view is too zoomed out compared to the tight, well-framed crop of the source figure. This makes the stems and labels appear sparse and disconnected.
discrepancies: The label 'm' is floating in the middle of the grid lines instead of being positioned cleanly at the end of the m-axis.
The vertical axis label 'b2,2[n,m]' is rotated and placed near the top-center instead of being aligned with the vertical axis on the left.
The grid structure has extra outer box lines that make it look cluttered compared to the clean, minimal back-wall grids of the source.
The vertical axis tick labels (0, 1, 2, 4) are shifted and floating awkwardly instead of sitting next to the tick marks on the main axis line.
evaluation.notes: The 3D stem plot correctly represents the 2D binomial filter coefficients, but the visual execution is cluttered with poorly placed, floating HTML labels and a camera view that is too zoomed out.
action_items: Adjust the setCameraView settings to zoom in closer, matching the tight crop and perspective of the original textbook image.
Reposition the 'm', 'n', and 'b2,2[n,m]' labels so they sit cleanly outside the grid, matching the source placement exactly.
Refine the grid lines to only draw the back walls and bottom floor, removing the extra bounding box lines to reduce visual clutter.
Align the numerical tick labels (-1, 0, 1) and (0, 1, 2, 4) precisely next to their corresponding tick marks.

gpt-5.5.failure_modes_analysis: The generated plot uses the correct blue stems/markers and gray 3D grid, and it includes code for the main labels. However, the rendered view is rotated/mirrored relative to the source: the vertical axis and b-label appear near the middle/right instead of on the left, and the base axes are oriented differently. The framing is also oversized/cropped, causing important axis labeling such as the n label and some ticks to be absent or not clearly visible in the screenshot.
gpt-5.5.failure_modes_reasoning: Missing-Labels applies because some important annotations visible in the source, especially the n-axis label and upper/tick labels, are not clearly visible in the rendered screenshot. Camera-Wrong applies because the initial viewpoint is substantially different from the source, with the vertical axis on the wrong side and the plot orientation rotated. Scale-Wrong applies because the scene is zoomed/framed too large and partially cropped compared with the compact source figure.
gpt-5.5.geometry_accuracy_analysis: The scene constructs a real 3D stem plot: a gridded floor/back/right wireframe frame, black axes and tick marks, nine blue vertical stems at a 3×3 lattice, and blue spherical markers with heights matching the binomial coefficient pattern 1-2-1 / 2-4-2 / 1-2-1. The n, m, coefficient, and tick labels are present as HTML overlays. The geometry is clean, symmetric, and genuinely 3D rather than a flat perspective-baked drawing; any issues are minor label/axis styling choices rather than missing geometry.
gpt-5.5.geometry_accuracy_reasoning: All major geometric elements of the 3D coefficient stem plot are present with correct topology, positions, and relative heights, satisfying the score-5 rubric.
gpt-5.5.interactivity_usability_analysis: The code includes OrbitControls for camera drag/zoom, which works but does not count as a meaningful interaction. A hidden Reset View button has a click handler, but the button is display:none so it is not usable. The meaningful interaction is object-based hover/click: blue coefficient markers are registered for raycast hover tooltips showing b₂,₂[n,m] values and clicks post a popup explaining the coefficient; the whole sample group is also registered for a general explanation when clicked. There are no sliders, toggles, animations, or guided step-through demo, and the small transparent UI area does not cover the geometry.
gpt-5.5.interactivity_usability_reasoning: There is one functional pedagogical object-inspection interaction via hover/click explanations, with no guided demo or parameter controls, so it fits rubric score 3.
gpt-5.5.faithfulness_analysis: The rendered scene contains a similar blue 3D stem plot with light gray gridlines, but the first frame is much wider than the square source, heavily zoomed/cropped, and the plot fills the canvas rather than sitting with the same compact margins. The camera angle and axis placement differ substantially: the vertical axis and label appear near the center/right instead of along the left edge, markers are much larger, and portions of the axes/grid are cut off or stretched compared with the original.
gpt-5.5.faithfulness_reasoning: Although the general stem-plot idea is recognizable, the aspect ratio, zoom/crop, marker scale, margins, and axis placement differ enough that it matches well under 65% as a drop-in replacement.
gpt-5.5.label_quality_analysis: Source labels include the main vertical axis label b_{2,2}[n,m], axis labels n and m, and numeric tick labels on the axes including z ticks 0, 1, 2, 4 and horizontal-axis ticks such as -1, 0, 1. In the generated figure, b_{2,2}[n,m] appears but is smaller and repositioned on the right side rather than clearly along the left vertical axis; the n label is not clearly visible, and the m label appears tiny and cluttered near the axis intersection rather than placed like the source. Some numeric tick labels are present, but several are missing or obscured/misaligned, and the overall font scale is much smaller than the source.
gpt-5.5.label_quality_reasoning: Most labels are missing, misplaced, cluttered, or too small relative to the source, so the label quality fits score 2.
gpt-5.5.concept_accuracy_analysis: The figure illustrates a 3×3 2-D binomial filter coefficient stem plot b₂,₂[n,m] with values arranged as [1,2,1]ᵀ[1,2,1], including n and m axes, coefficient-height axis, and tick labels. The hover/click explanations are figure-specific and correctly identify each coefficient value, the separable binomial-product structure, the largest center coefficient, total sum/DC gain of 16, and normalization by 16 for brightness preservation.
gpt-5.5.concept_accuracy_reasoning: All displayed concepts and interactive explanations are accurate, specific to the binomial-filter stem plot, and contain no evident misinformation.
